text: Photoelectrochemical (PEC) epoxidation is a highly sought-after sustainable method for catalyst-assisted synthesis of pharmaceuticals, and epoxy resins. Here, we report on a PEC setup which uses a Br⁻/BrO⁻ redox mediator on an α-Fe₂O₃ (hematite) photoanode. The PEC setup enables the catalytic selective epoxidation of a range of alkenes at selectivities above 99% and at faradaic efficiencies of up to 82 ± 4%, which are the highest reported values so far and clearly outperform all current state-of-the-art electrochemical and photoelectrochemical epoxidation works. Water as sole source of oxygen proves to be evidence of a non-radical oxygen atom transfer (OAT) mechanism occurring at the hematite surface. Br⁻ is oxidized by OAT to BrO⁻ and the BrO⁻ then acts as a catalyst in delivering an overall non-quantum-mechanical atom transfer mechanism, which leads to an efficient, feasible and extremely selective synthesis of the targeted epoxides, as well as green hydrogen production.
